rulings.Case Title: UNFUND CCR PARTNERS VS. YESSICA E GUERRA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Defendant'S Name On Judgment And Renewal Of Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAR-05-2026. LINE 1. PLAINTIFF UNFUND CCR PARTNERS' Motion To Amend Defendant'S Name On Judgment And Renewal Of Judgment. Judgment creditor Unfund CCR Partners' motion to amend the defendant's name on the judgment and renewal of judgment is granted. The name "Yessica E. Vargas" shall be added as an additional name of the judgment debtor on the judgment and renewal of judgment. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH WILLIAMS VS. SERVE ALL, HELP ALL, INC., DBA TEH NONPROFIT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Orders Deeming Requests For Admissions Admitted And Orders Granting Motions For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAR-05-2026. LINE 13. PLAINTIFF KENNETH WILLIAMS' Motion For Relief From Orders Deeming Requests For Admissions Admitted And Orders Granting Motions For Judgment On The Pleadings. Hearing required. The tentative ruling posted on February 27 stated: Plaintiff Kenneth Williams' motion for relief from orders deeming requests for admissions admitted and orders granting motions for judgment on the pleadings is denied. The sole grounds for this motion is the asserted misconduct of the A1 Law Group constitutes extrinsic fraud. However, the record shows that plaintiff was aware of and had an opportunity to oppose the motions which he seeks to have set aside and thus any fraud perpetrated against him is intrinsic fraud which is not a basis to set aside otherwise valid court orders. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: MHYF HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. CHRIS V. KELLY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAR-05-2026. LINE 14. DEFENDANTS CHRIS KELLY, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS CO-TRUSTEE OF THE KELLY FAMILY TRUST, and LARA KELLY's MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Defendants Chris and Lara Kelly's motion to quash deposition subpoena to Kensington Capital Management LLC is granted in full. Kensington is ordered not to produce any of the documents sought by the subpoena. Plaintiff MYHF Holdings. LLC has not shown that any of the financial documents it seeks are directly relevant to any of its claims nor has MYHF shown that any of the non-financial documents it seeks meet the standard for discovery relevance. Despite its very lengthy fourth amended complaint, MYHF has not identified a single allegation in its operative complaint as to which the financial documents it seeks are directly relevant or that the non-financial documents it seeks meet the standard for discovery relevance. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. Continued to March 18, 2026 to give the parties an opportunity to meet and confer in an effort to resolve all or some of the issues raised by the motion. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAR-05-2026. LINE 15. DEFENDANTS SWORD HEALTH, INC., SWORD HEALTH, S.A., and VIRGILIO BENTO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery. Hearing required. The tentative ruling posted on February 27, 2026 stated: Defendants' motion to compel further responses to discovery is granted in part. If they have not already done so, no later than March 10, 2026 plaintiffs must produce all responsive documents for the five time periods which they now contend that defendants made equity offers. No later than April 30, 2026 plaintiffs must identify any further equity offers which they intend to seek relief at trial and produce all responsive documents for any of those equity offers which they had not previously produced all responsive documents. Plaintiffs may not seek relief at trial as to any equity offers that they have not produced all responsive documents by April 30, 2026. rulings.Case Title: JULIA PADILLA VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Court Order Compelling Pmq Deposition; Request For Additional Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Kexin Zheng
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAR-05-2026. LINE 6. PLAINTIFF JULIA PADILLA's Motion To Enforce Court Order Compelling Pmq Deposition; Request For Additional Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff Julia Padilla's motion to enforce court order compelling PMQ deposition and for monetary sanctions is denied. The portion of the motion seeking an order compelling the PMQ deposition appears to be moot because the parties appear to have agreed on a date for the deposition. The portion of the motion seeking monetary sanctions lacks merit because the motion papers fail to explain why plaintiff was unable to confirm a deposition date of January 26 per the timetable set by defendant or why it took plaintiff six days to confirm that date. Moreover, the plaintiff failed to pursue this deposition date issue with diligence. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: SAHIRA VIERA ET AL VS. 735 O' FARRELL I7, LP, A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set One From Plaintiff Carlos Viera; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Carlos Viera; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Carlos Viera And His Counsel Of Record Thomas Purtell And Anthony Chiosso In The Amount Of $1,560.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Justin M. Narvisi
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAR-05-2026. LINE 7. 1-DEFENDANT 735 O' FARRELL I7, LP's, A DELAWARE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set One From Plaintiff Carlos Viera; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Carlos Viera; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Carlos Viera And His Counsel Of Record Thomas Purtell And Anthony Chiosso In The Amount Of $1,560.00. Defendant 735 O'Farrell 17, LP's motion to compel further responses to its first sets of form and special interrogatories from plaintiff Carlos Viera and for monetary sanctions is denied. The amended responses to both sets of interrogatories served on or about December 10, 2025 are sufficient, particularly given the large number of interrogatories and the overlapping nature of those interrogatories. The amended responses do not prevent or hamper defendants from preparing for trial. If defendants wish further information about plaintiffs' claims, there are other discovery devices that they may avail themselves of. No monetary sanctions are awarded to either side since both sides acted with substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAR-05-2026. LINE 7. 2-DEFENDANT GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.'s, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set One From Plaintiff Carlos Viera; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Carlos Viera And His Counsel Of Reocrd Thomas Purtell And Anthony Chiosso In The Amount Of $1560.00. Defendant Greentree Property Management, Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to its first sets of form and special interrogatories from plaintiff Carlos Viera and for monetary sanctions is denied. The amended responses to both sets of interrogatories served on or about December 10, 2025 are sufficient, particularly given the large number of interrogatories and the overlapping nature of those interrogatories. The amended responses do not prevent or hamper defendants from preparing for trial. If defendants wish further information about plaintiffs' claims, there are other discovery devices that they may avail themselves of. No monetary sanctions are awarded to either side since both sides acted with substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/HEK)

LINE 9. 4-DEFENDANT VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff Cristina Gabriela Sanchez's Initial Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One; And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel Of Record. Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.'s motion to compel plaintiff Cristina Sanchez to respond to set one form interrogatories is granted. Volkswagen has shown that Cristina Sanchez has not responded to the set one form interrogatories. No later than March 25, 2026 Cristina Sanchez must serve verified code-compliant responses without objections to all set one form interrogatories. Due to her failure to respond to the form interrogatories or this motion, no later than April 17, 2026 Cristina Sanchez must pay reasonable monetary sanctions of $455 to Volkswagen. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: AMANDA SILK VS. NAIM NAZZAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Verified Responses And Further Production To Request For Production, Set One; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAR-05-2026. LINE 11. PLAINTIFF AMANDA SILK's, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA SILK AND LACE OCCASIONS, Motion To Compel Verified Responses And Further Production To Request For Production, Set One; Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff Amanda Silk's motion to compel verified responses and further production to her set one requests for production and for monetary sanctions is granted in part. No later than March 18, 2026 defendant Helen Nazzal must serve a verification for its responses to the set one requests. The court credits Nazzal's untimely opposition that all responsive documents in her possession, custody and control have been produced. That opposition, however, does not address the portion of the motion seeking a verification. Silk's sole ground for "modest" monetary sanctions is that she "was forced to expend time and effort preparing this motion." The California Supreme Court has held that this is not a permissible basis for discovery monetary sanctions. (Musaelian v. Adams (2009) 45 Cal. 4th 512). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY SUN VS. THE IVY AT GOLDEN GATE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion For Stay
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAR-05-2026. LINE 12. DEFENDANTS OAKMONT MANAGEMENT GROUP LLC, WELLTOWER TRS HOLDCO LLC, WELLTOWER OP LLC, WELLTOWER INC, IVY LIVING LLC, and WELL OAK TENANT, LLC's, DBA THE IVY AT GOLDEN GATE, Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion For Stay. Defendants' petition to compel arbitration and motion for stay of this case pending completion of arbitration proceedings are denied. The evidence shows that Sandy Stefancic, not Betty Sun, put her initials at the end of section m on page 31 of the residence agreement. Defendants have not provided any evidence showing that when Stefancic put her initials at the end of section m she had either actual or ostensible authority to do so on behalf of Sun. Accordingly, defendants have failed to meet their burden of showing that there is a valid agreement to arbitrate binding on Sun. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
